question: For Acme Manufacturing, what is the per-unit throughput (selling price minus totally variable cost) of "Product A", in dollars?

For Acme Manufacturing this period, which work center is the constraint (the bottleneck resource that does not have enough capacity to meet full market demand for every product)? Answer with just the work center's name.
For Acme Manufacturing, which product has the HIGHEST throughput per constraint-minute (T/CU) at the constraint work center? Answer with just the product's name.

For Acme Manufacturing, which product has the HIGHEST per-unit throughput in absolute dollar terms (selling price minus totally variable cost), ignoring how much constraint capacity it uses? Answer with just the product's name.

For Acme Manufacturing, in the optimal product mix (produce the highest-T/CU products first, up to their market demand, until the constraint's available capacity is used up), how many units of "Product D" should be produced?
For Acme Manufacturing, what is the total throughput (in dollars) generated by the optimal product mix this period, after allocating the constraint's available capacity by T/CU priority?
For Acme Manufacturing, what is the net profit for the period (total throughput of the optimal mix minus operating expenses), in dollars?

A traditional cost accountant at Acme Manufacturing recommends prioritizing production of whichever product has the highest throughput (margin) per unit in absolute dollar terms. Would that recommended product be the SAME product that throughput accounting recommends prioritizing first (the highest T/CU product at the constraint)? Answer Yes or No.
